# Join Our Team

## Current Vacancies

Thank you for your interest in joining the team at Abbeyfield Bristol & Keynsham Society! We appreciate your enthusiasm and welcome your inquiry. All staff members are required to undergo a DBS check and provide relevant references.

# Day Support Kift Lodge, Hanham

[Be the Sunshine in Someone’s Day! Looking for a fulfilling daytime role? Join us as a Day Support worker and help make our supported house a warm, welcoming place for older people. Day shifts will include: Assisting with lunch service and clearing up Keeping communal areas clean and tidy Supporting residents with daily needs Dealing with resident and house emergencies, and liaising with domiciliary services and family Helping with light admin and ensuring the house runs smoothly £14.70/hour | 🕓 Shifts available are 9.00am – 5.00pm Friday. You will be working in our supportive, friendly team in a beautiful Abbeyfield house in Hanham, Bristol. If you’re compassionate, reliable, and love a good chat over a cuppa, we’d love to meet you!](http://www.abbeyfield-bristol.co.uk/cookhanham)
  Be the Sunshine in Someone’s Day!
  Looking for a fulfilling daytime role? Join us as a Day Support worker and help make our supported house a warm, welcoming place for older people.
  Day shifts will include:
  Assisting with lunch service and clearing up
  Keeping communal areas clean and tidy
  Supporting residents with daily needs
  Dealing with resident and house emergencies, and liaising with domiciliary services and family
  Helping with light admin and ensuring the house runs smoothly
  £14.70/hour | 🕓 Shifts available are 9.00am – 5.00pm Friday.
  You will be working in our supportive, friendly team in a beautiful Abbeyfield house in Hanham, Bristol.
  If you’re compassionate, reliable, and love a good chat over a cuppa, we’d love to meet you!

Find your new home with Abbeyfield Bristol & Keynsham

# Abbeyfield Bristol and Keynsham Trustee

Join Our Board: Make a Real Difference at Abbeyfield Bristol & Keynsham

Are you ready to use your skills for something that truly matters? Abbeyfield Bristol & Keynsham isn’t just another charity; we’re a community. We provide supported housing and a sense of belonging for older people, and we’re on a mission to do even more.

As we prepare to celebrate our 60th Anniversary, we are looking for new trustees to join our board.

The Role of a Trustee

As a Trustee, you will serve on a voluntary basis, playing a vital role in our organisation’s strategic direction and governance. You will work with the dedicated Board and our Chief Executive Officer to ensure we achieve our mission and deliver on our new strategic plan.

Who We Are Looking For

We are seeking passionate individuals who are committed to the welfare of older people. While we welcome applicants from all backgrounds, we are currently looking to strengthen our Board with specific skills in any of the following areas:

- Finance
- VCSE and Social Housing
- Legal
- Human Resources
- Marketing & Communications

Commitment

- Term: Trustees serve a 3-year term, after which they may be re-elected.
- Time: The Board meets quarterly to set strategic direction and monitor performance. Trustees will also be asked to be part of a sub groups when required and are encouraged to visit the Houses.

If you are interested in this voluntary position and believe you have skills and passion to contribute to Abbeyfield Bristol & Keynsham, please contact us.

We would be delighted to hear from you.

To apply:

Please send your CV and covering letter explaining why you would like to become a trustee for Abbeyfield Bristol and Keynsham to e-mail@abbeyfield-bristol.co.uk.

To learn more or arrange a friendly chat, email Hayley Richards, Chair of Trustees at e-mail@abbeyfield-bristol.co.uk or call 0117 973 6997.

The deadline for applications is Friday 31st October.

## Apply Now
